subject: Re: Questions regarding CTF estimation in WarpTools
body:
Hi Luis,

- both procedures do provide defocus values for each tilt image
- frame series estimation adds spatial resolution which is useful
- tilt series estimation provides a more robust estimate for each image by
constraining parameters across the whole TS

I recommend reading the methods section of the Warp paper :-)

Cheers,

Alister

On Thu, 30 Jan 2025 at 09:34, Luis Carreira <luismenezescarreira@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Dear Alister,
>
> Thanks a lot for your answers, as always very helpful.
>
> "You can see per-tilt defocus values in the tilt series XML files"
>
> You mean under this header on my tiltseries XML file?:
> </CTF>
> <GridCTF Width="1" Height="1" Depth="58" MarginX="0" MarginY="0"
> MarginZ="0">
> <Node X="0" Y="0" Z="0" Value="2.812471" />
> <Node X="0" Y="0" Z="1" Value="2.8129659" />
> ...
>
> I am a bit puzzled here because the frames series XML file that each frame
> has also has a defocus value, for example:
> <Param Name="Defocus" Value="2.763969" />
>
> Thanks again,
>
> Luis
>
> On Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 5:10 PM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Luis,
>>
>> The frame series and tilt series ctf fitting procedures are independent.
>> We do the frame series estimate with spatial resolution 2x2 so that we
>> can measure how defocus changes across the tilt axis, allowing us to ensure
>> the correct defocus handedness in the reconstruction/particles exported.
>>
>> The tilt series CTF fitting procedure estimates a single defocus value
>> per tilt image so cannot be used to check the defocus handedness.
>> You can see per-tilt defocus values in the tilt series XML files, these
>> values were previously visible in the Warp desktop GUI and will be visible
>> in any GUI we make moving forwards :)
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> On Wed, 29 Jan 2025 at 07:25, Luis Carreira <
>> luismenezescarreira@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Dear all,
>>>
>>> I am writing this message to understand a bit more how CTF estimation is
>>> performed in Warp. I have followed the tutorial in:
>>>
>>> https://warpem.github.io/warp/user_guide/warptools/quick_start_warptools_tilt_series/#download-the-data
>>>
>>> In this tutorial we perform two CTF estimation steps, during frame
>>> series and tilt series. My first question is: does the second CTF
>>> estimation take any info from the first?
>>>
>>> My second question is: is there a way to have access to the defocus per
>>> tilt image? As far as I am aware, the WarpTools filter_quality for the
>>> frames series only gives the histogram and the WarpTools filter_quality for
>>> the tilt series only gives the average defocus of the tilt series.
>>>
>>> Thanks,
>>>
>>> Luis
>>>
>>> --
>>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google
>>> Groups "Warp" group.
>>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send
>>> an email to warp-em+unsubscribe@googlegroups.com.

>>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/abaacbb3-290d-429c-8566-b12440a3101dn%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>>> .
>>>
>>
